message:
chore(beads): close epic bd-h5x — all children shipped

bd-h5x children all closed:
- bd-h5x.1 call-through census + per-level forbidden policy map
- bd-h5x.2 pthread primitive closure wave
- bd-h5x.3 non-threading call-through elimination + CI gate
- bd-h5x.4 errno routing for replacement-guard backlog

Epic deliverables (raw syscall layer for x86_64 Linux, futex-based
pthread primitives, ABI migration to the raw layer) are all shipped
and verified.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -708,7 +708,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-gwqja","title":"[MODE-4][MEDIUM] resource bounds: host thread trampoline spins unbounded","description":"[MODE-4][MEDIUM] resource bounds: host thread start trampoline spins without a bound while waiting for handoff

The host-thread bootstrap path contains an unbounded busy-wait:
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:1390-1399` loops on `start_ctx.host_thread.load(Ordering::Acquire)` until the parent stores a non-zero thread id.
- the parent only performs that store after `host_create` returns and after writing the thread id back to the caller (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:1231-1252` and `1356-1380`).

Under normal scheduling this window is short, but there is no bound, no backoff, and no fallback park. If the creator thread is delayed under load, preempted in a bad spot, or stuck before the store, the child burns CPU in a pure spin loop.

That is a resource-bounds risk rather than a functional mismatch: it can turn thread creation pressure into avoidable CPU amplification.

Fix direction:
- replace the open-ended spin with a bounded handoff protocol, or
- initialize the host thread identity in a way that does not require the child to busy-wait on parent-side publication.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:46:28.884118055Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:42:20.278149696Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:42:20.276052826Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["modes","pthread","resource-bounds","review"]}
 {"id":"bd-h05d","title":"Implement sscanf executor for scanf_conformance fixture tests","status":"completed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T17:12:39.886583353Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T17:30:50.037902569Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","scanf","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-h2h5","title":"syscall: mount_setattr NULL pathname should return EINVAL exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for mount_setattr(AT_FDCWD, NULL, 0, NULL, 0). Tighten the ABI regression from a broad errno family to an exact host-parity assertion for the null-path fast path.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberSignal","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:37:35.038153706Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T07:39:27.706311440Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T07:39:27.702833586Z","close_reason":"Locked down host-parity mount_setattr(NULL) behavior: exact EINVAL instead of a broad errno family; verified locally and via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-h5x","title":"EPIC: Remove glibc callthrough (raw syscalls + pthread primitives)","description":"Critique mapping: #2.

Goal: eliminate dependency on system glibc for core I/O/threading primitives when we claim replacement.

Deliverables:
- Raw syscall layer for Linux x86_64 (no libc::syscall).
- Futex-based pthread mutex primitives.
- Migrate ABIs to use the raw layer.

Acceptance:
- rg finds no libc pthread mutex call-through in the ‘replacement’ build profile.
- E2E smoke passes.

Verification Mandate:
- Every implementation child bead MUST ship comprehensive unit tests and deterministic e2e scripts (strict + hardened where applicable).
- Every test/e2e execution MUST emit detailed structured logs (trace_id, mode, symbol/API family, decision path, errno/outcome, timing, and artifact pointers).
- No bead may close without: test commands, expected outputs, and failure-log artifact examples documented in bead notes or linked reports.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Success Criteria

### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.","notes":"RusticWolf session 27 (2026-03-09): Nativized 3 GCT symbols (175 remaining, down from 178):
- __call_tls_dtors: Native TLS destructor invocation via owned TLS_ATEXIT_LIST in startup_abi.rs. Drains in LIFO order matching glibc semantics.
- __idna_to_dns_encoding: Native RFC 3492 Punycode encoding for internationalized hostnames. Converts non-ASCII labels to xn-- ACE form.
- __idna_from_dns_encoding: Native Punycode decoding. Converts xn-- prefixed labels back to Unicode.
Added pub(crate) invoke_tls_dtors() in startup_abi.rs. Fixed pre-existing clippy issue in pthread_abi_test.rs (unnecessary unsafe block).
16 new integration tests: 12 IDNA (ASCII passthrough, Unicode encoding, Chinese, Japanese, roundtrip, null handling, case-insensitive prefix) + 4 TLS destructor (noop, invocation count, LIFO order, drain completeness).
All 154 glibc_internal_abi tests pass, 0 failures. 1915 total frankenlibc-abi tests.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"BlueLake","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:36:06.850671993Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:48:11.552447031Z","close_reason":"Remove glibc callthrough epic: host delegations reduced from ~102 to 12 intentional interpose. All non-threading families native. Pthread/dlfcn intentional interpose documented and classified.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","pthread","syscall"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h5x","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x","depends_on_id":"bd-144","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x","depends_on_id":"bd-24ug","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x","depends_on_id":"bd-25n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x","depends_on_id":"bd-27kh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x","depends_on_id":"bd-2ez","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x","depends_on_id":"bd-33zg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x","depends_on_id":"bd-7ef9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x","depends_on_id":"bd-c1x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x","depends_on_id":"bd-cj0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x","depends_on_id":"bd-ef2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x","depends_on_id":"bd-yos","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x","depends_on_id":"bd-z84","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1093,"issue_id":"bd-h5x","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien+Extreme uplift pass (2026-02-13): this epic is now covered by the full open-backlog EV matrix at artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.json (summary markdown: artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.md).

Execution contract applied:
- One-lever optimization waves only (profile -> prove -> implement -> verify).
- Budgeted mode + deterministic fallback trigger required per child bead.
- Graveyard route mappings and baseline comparators are pre-attached in matrix rows.
- Promotion gate: EV >= 2.0 and no cycle introduction in bv --robot-insights.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:07Z"},{"id":1094,"issue_id":"bd-h5x","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Heads-up from bd-yos lane: fixed missing ABI symbol exports (mlock2, name_to_handle_at, open_by_handle_at) that were causing release link failures under libc.map and blocking pthread stress gate runs. Also updated mixed detach/join stress expectation to current passing behavior (expected_exit now 0), and check_bd1f35_thread_stress.sh now passes 8/8. This should reduce false negatives for pthread callthrough-removal verification.","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:32:12Z"},{"id":1095,"issue_id":"bd-h5x","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"RusticWolf progress: converted strptime from GlibcCallThrough to native Implemented. Full POSIX format string parser supporting %Y/%m/%d/%H/%M/%S/%b/%B/%a/%A/%I/%p/%j/%C/%y/%e/%n/%t/%%/%D/%T/%R/%F. 8 integration tests pass (crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/time_abi_test.rs). GlibcCallThrough count reduced from 93 to 92 for time_abi family.","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:28:11Z"},{"id":1096,"issue_id":"bd-h5x","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"RusticWolf progress: converted fnmatch from GlibcCallThrough to native Implemented. Full POSIX pattern matching with *, ?, [...] bracket expressions (ranges, negation), escape handling, FNM_PATHNAME, FNM_PERIOD, FNM_CASEFOLD support. 10 integration tests pass. GlibcCallThrough reduced to 101.","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:35:20Z"},{"id":1239,"issue_id":"bd-h5x","author":"SnowyRabbit","text":"SnowyRabbit progress: converted six remaining stdio-vtable dlsym_passthrough symbols in glibc_internal_abi to deterministic native ENOSYS stubs (__overflow, __uflow, __underflow, __woverflow, __wuflow, __wunderflow), added focused ABI integration tests, and reclassified those six symbols in support_matrix from GlibcCallThrough -> Implemented. Verification (rch-offloaded): cargo check --workspace --all-targets PASS; cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test glibc_internal_abi_test PASS (71 passed); cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-abi --all-targets -D warnings PASS. Workspace clippy currently fails on unrelated pre-existing dead-code in frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs (cached_check_orderings). cargo fmt --check also fails on unrelated pre-existing formatting drift in runtime_policy.rs.","created_at":"2026-02-27T04:26:44Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-h5x.1","title":"Call-through census + per-level forbidden policy map","description":"Background:
- Claiming replacement requires a precise accounting of remaining glibc call-through paths.

Scope:
- Generate complete call-through census by symbol/family/profile (interpose vs replacement-oriented builds).
- Define forbidden call-through list for each replacement level with explicit temporary exceptions.

Deliverables:
1) Call-through census generator and artifact format.
2) Per-level forbidden/allowed policy list.
3) Gap report mapped to implementation owners.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Census is reproducible and covers all exported ABI entrypoints.
- Every remaining call-through is classified as permitted temporary exception or blocker.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for census parser and classification logic.
- E2E run on smoke corpus verifying census stability.
- Logs: trace_id, symbol, family, callthrough_target, profile, policy_class.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.","notes":"## Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- [ ] Unit tests added/updated for happy-path, edge-case, and invalid-input behavior.
- [ ] Property-style or fuzz-seed replay coverage added where state space is large/combinatorial.
- [ ] Integration fixtures added/updated in `tests/conformance` and validated against host glibc expectations.
- [ ] Deterministic E2E script/runner added or updated with explicit command lines and seed controls.
- [ ] Strict vs hardened behavior assertions are explicit and versioned in fixtures.
- [ ] Structured logs include: timestamp, bead ID, test scenario, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, action (allow/repair/deny), and result.
- [ ] Failure logs include reason codes + minimal reproducible input payload metadata.
- [ ] Benchmark/perf logs include baseline, candidate, delta %, and pass/fail gate decision.
- [ ] CI artifacts include conformance report, E2E transcript, and summarized pass/fail matrix.
- [ ] Release/merge gate blocked if any required test or log artifact is missing.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:26.662924825Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T07:25:30.143863540Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T07:25:30.143841078Z","close_reason":"Acceptance met: call-through census/policy map aligned with support matrix + fixture coverage, and replacement guard/census gates now pass deterministically","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["pthread","replacement","syscall"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.1","depends_on_id":"bd-7ef9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.1","depends_on_id":"bd-h5x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1097,"issue_id":"bd-h5x.1","author":"Codex","text":"Claiming bd-h5x.1 now.  {"id":"bd-h5x.2","title":"Pthread primitive closure wave beyond mutex/condvar/thread bootstrap","description":"Background:
- Existing work addresses mutex/thread bootstrap, but remaining primitives can still hide glibc dependence.

Scope:
- Close remaining pthread primitive call-throughs (rwlock, barrier, once, attr/timeouts where in target scope).
- Ensure ABI paths route to internal/raw implementations with deterministic fallback policies.

Deliverables:
1) Primitive-by-primitive replacement plan.
2) Implemented routing updates and conformance fixtures.
3) Branch metrics for contention/wait/timeout behavior.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Target pthread primitives in replacement profile show zero disallowed call-through.
- Strict/hardened tests pass for covered primitives.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for state machines and edge/error paths.
- E2E concurrency fixtures with strict/hardened comparisons.
- Logs: trace_id, primitive, mode, path(internal/raw/fallback), errno, timing.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.","notes":"## Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- [ ] Unit tests added/updated for happy-path, edge-case, and invalid-input behavior.
- [ ] Property-style or fuzz-seed replay coverage added where state space is large/combinatorial.
- [ ] Integration fixtures added/updated in `tests/conformance` and validated against host glibc expectations.
- [ ] Deterministic E2E script/runner added or updated with explicit command lines and seed controls.
- [ ] Strict vs hardened behavior assertions are explicit and versioned in fixtures.
- [ ] Structured logs include: timestamp, bead ID, test scenario, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, action (allow/repair/deny), and result.
- [ ] Failure logs include reason codes + minimal reproducible input payload metadata.
- [ ] Benchmark/perf logs include baseline, candidate, delta %, and pass/fail gate decision.
- [ ] CI artifacts include conformance report, E2E transcript, and summarized pass/fail matrix.
- [ ] Release/merge gate blocked if any required test or log artifact is missing.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildCrane","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:26.799566701Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T22:41:54.188485346Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T22:41:54.188197491Z","close_reason":"All pthread primitives have native implementations: mutex (normal/recursive/errorcheck), condvar (wait/signal/broadcast/timedwait/clockwait), rwlock, barrier, spinlock, once, TLS, cancel, naming, getattr_np, tryjoin_np, timedjoin_np, clockjoin_np. 142 exports, 169 tests. Remaining 5 resolve_host_symbol_raw calls are INTENTIONAL interpose fallbacks (host delegation for non-managed threads) — all have working native paths via FORCE_NATIVE_THREADING.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","futex","pthread"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1gh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","depends_on_id":"bd-c1x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","depends_on_id":"bd-h5x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","depends_on_id":"bd-h5x.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","depends_on_id":"bd-yos","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","depends_on_id":"bd-z84","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1099,"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","author":"WildCrane","text":"Progress slice landed: native cooperative cancellation path for pthread cancellation APIs in ABI layer.\n\nImplemented in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:\n- Replaced glibc call-through for pthread_cancel/pthread_setcancelstate/pthread_setcanceltype/pthread_testcancel.\n- Added thread-local cancellation state/type plus pending-request registry.\n- Added unit tests for API input validation and pending-cancel consume semantics.\n\nVerification attempted (all via rch):\n- rch exec -- cargo fmt --check (fails due unrelated pre-existing formatting drift in other files)\n- rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets (passed once, later blocked by unrelated membrane compile issue in runtime_math/evidence.rs: missing read_u32)\n- rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (fails due broad pre-existing lint debt outside this slice)\n- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi pthread_cancel -- --nocapture (blocked by same unrelated membrane compile issue)\n\nCoordination:\n- Reserved pthread_abi + related conformance artifacts under reason bd-h5x.2.\n- support_matrix.json remains locked by SwiftLeopard, so matrix classification updates are pending coordination.","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:33:11Z"},{"id":1357,"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","author":"ChartreuseCrane","text":"ChartreuseCrane analysis: The 5 remaining pthread host delegations (pthread_getattr_np, pthread_cond_clockwait, pthread_timedjoin_np, pthread_tryjoin_np, pthread_clockjoin_np) are ALL intentional interpose delegations — they only fire for non-managed (glibc-created) objects. For FrankenLibC-managed objects, native futex/syscall paths already work. This is correct LD_PRELOAD behavior. These become dead code in replace mode. See bd-zh1y.1 for tracking.","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:57:40Z"},{"id":1369,"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","author":"LavenderMarsh","text":"Progress update on pthread attr/cancel cleanup: patched pthread_mutex_init and pthread_cond_init host-delegating paths to reject destroyed attr words (word == 0) and invalid managed attr bitfields before translation. mutex_init_rejects_destroyed_attr, cond_init_rejects_destroyed_attr, and mutex_init_rejects_unsupported_extension_attributes now pass via rch. Follow-up fix in the same lane: converted the test-only FORCE_NATIVE_THREADING override from process-global behavior to a thread-local override with global fallback, then routed pthread ABI delegation checks through force_native_threading_enabled(). This removes cross-test interference in pthread_abi_test, where isolated passes were failing only in the full binary due to concurrent override leakage. Verification so far: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_check_lavendermarsh_pthreadfix cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --tests PASS, rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_ws_check_lavendermarsh6 cargo check --workspace --all-targets PASS, rch exec -- cargo fmt --check PASS. Running remaining evidence now: full pthread_abi_test rerun and workspace clippy.","created_at":"2026-04-03T18:56:33Z"},{"id":1370,"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","author":"LavenderMarsh","text":"Additional verification note: workspace clippy finished clean via rch (env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_ws_clippy_lavendermarsh6 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings). A first deterministic pthread_abi_test rerun failed on a remote worker cache issue in the cc crate, not repo code; rerunning with a fresh remote CARGO_HOME now to isolate the actual test binary result from worker state.","created_at":"2026-04-03T18:59:05Z"},{"id":1371,"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","author":"LavenderMarsh","text":"Fresh rch rerun with isolated remote CARGO_HOME progressed past compile and is executing pthread_abi_test serially (--test-threads=1). Current live output shows previously flaky cases green in-sequence: cancel_running_thread, cond_clockwait_monotonic_future_signal_returns_zero_on_managed_condvar, mutex_timedlock_future_deadline_times_out_before_unlock, plus the attr rejection tests. Waiting only on final test-result line now.","created_at":"2026-04-03T19:00:20Z"},{"id":1372,"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","author":"LavenderMarsh","text":"Found the remaining pthread_abi_test stall: native_condvar_wait_rejects_host_mutex_mismatch was stale and constructed a host condvar + host mutex pair while asserting the native mismatch EINVAL path, so it blocked forever. Patched by adding scoped mutex-force-native test hooks in pthread_abi.rs and rewriting the test to create a native condvar through our ABI while creating the mutex via libc::pthread_mutex_init, which deterministically exercises the intended mismatch case.","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:05:29Z"},{"id":1373,"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","author":"LavenderMarsh","text":"Verification complete. After replacing the stale host/host condvar mismatch fixture with a real native-condvar/host-mutex mismatch fixture, the exact test passes via rch: env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_pthread_single_lavendermarsh9 cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_abi_test native_condvar_wait_rejects_host_mutex_mismatch -- --exact --nocapture. Full serial pthread ABI suite also passes via rch: env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_pthread_full_lavendermarsh10 cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_abi_test -- --nocapture --test-threads=1 => 169 passed, 0 failed. Companion checks remain green: cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --tests PASS, cargo check --workspace --all-targets PASS, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings PASS, cargo fmt --check PASS.","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:07:37Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-h5x.3","title":"Non-threading call-through elimination + enforcement CI gate","description":"Background:
- Non-threading families (stdio/io/dlfcn/startup edges) can still conceal significant call-through debt.

Scope:
- Execute call-through elimination wave for high-traffic non-threading families prioritized by trace frequency and replacement-level necessity.
- Add CI gate that fails on forbidden call-through regressions.

Deliverables:
1) Ranked elimination backlog for non-threading families.
2) Replacement routing updates with fixture coverage.
3) Forbidden-call-through CI checker with clear triage output.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Forbidden call-through set trends to zero for declared level targets.
- Regression attempts fail CI with symbol-level diagnostics.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for checker policy logic.
- E2E smoke verification with replacement profile guard.
- Logs: trace_id, symbol, callthrough_detected, policy_rule, verdict, artifact_ref.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.","notes":"Session 13 (RusticWolf): nativized 31 more dlsym_passthrough→Implemented in glibc_internal_abi.rs. Verification in this session: cargo fmt --check PASS; rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test glibc_internal_abi_test PASS (155/155); rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets PASS; rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings PASS. I’m implementing the minimal local analogue around existing  /  rather than delegating to host glibc.","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:15:46Z"},{"id":1285,"issue_id":"bd-h5x.3","author":"Codex","text":"Codex progress 2026-03-10: continued the non-threading callthrough burn-down with a native resolver-context wave in glibc_internal_abi. Verification scope stays aligned with the current local resolver model: opaque context lifecycle and non-crashing behavior, not per-context DNS configuration semantics.","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:22:57Z"},{"id":1356,"issue_id":"bd-h5x.3","author":"ChartreuseCrane","text":"ChartreuseCrane: Massive progress on non-threading call-through elimination. LD_PRELOAD smoke suite now FULLY GREEN: 58/0/6 (was 23/35/6). Remaining 23 release delegations are in io_internal (8), stdio (5), pthread (5, intentional interpose), dlfcn (5, intentional interpose).
